---
title: "Toshiba M200 Pen Driver"
date: 2009-03-17
forum: Hardware
---

### Post by Idire on 2009-03-17
Right, so I just installed Ubuntu 8.10 from Windows using the new option on the cd.

I am trying to get the touchscreen working, and I was looking at this page:

[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=855998](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=855998)


My xorg.conf file is this:

```
# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
# sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "InputDevice"
Identifier "Generic Keyboard"
Driver "kbd"
Option "XkbRules" "xorg"
Option "XkbModel" "pc105"
Option "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
Identifier "Configured Mouse"
Driver "mouse"
Option "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
Identifier "Synaptics Touchpad"
Driver "synaptics"
Option "SendCoreEvents" "true"
Option "Device" "/dev/psaux"
Option "Protocol" "auto-dev"
Option "HorizEdgeScroll" "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
Driver "wacom"
Identifier "stylus"
Option "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
Option "Type" "stylus"
Option "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
Endsection

Section "InputDevice"
Driver "wacom"
Identifier "eraser"
Option "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
Option "Type" "eraser"
Option "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
Driver "wacom"
Identifier "cursor"
Option "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
Option "Type" "cursor"
Option "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Screen"
Identifier "Default Screen"
Device "nVidia Corporation NV34M [GeForce FX Go5200 32M/64M]"
Monitor "Generic Monitor"
Defaultdepth 24
SubSection "Display"
Modes "1400x1050"
EndSubSection
EndSection


Section "Device"
Identifier "Configured Video Device"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
Identifier "Configured Monitor"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
Identifier "Default Screen"
Monitor "Configured Monitor"
Device "Configured Video Device"
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
Identifier "Default Layout"
Screen "Default Screen
InputDevice "Synaptics Touchpad"
Inputdevice	"stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
Inputdevice	"cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
Inputdevice	"eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
EndSection
```


I think I'm using Nvidia driver, but I've messed about with this conf file so much I'm not sure.

Tried  ```
setserial /dev/ttyS0 port 0x338 autoconfig
```

It said: 

```
/dev/ttyS0: No such device
```


I have installed the linuxwacom-0.8.2-2 drivers using ./install in terminal.



Any ideas?
Thanks

---

### Post by solar george on 2009-03-17
Try this xorg
```
# xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#                                                           
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.                                         
#                                                                           
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.           
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)                               
#                                                                           
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg    
# package.                                                                  
#                                                                           
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:                                         
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg                               

Section "Files"
EndSection     

#Section "InputDevice"
#       Identifier      "Generic Keyboard"
#       Driver          "kbd"             
#       Option          "CoreKeyboard"    
#       Option          "XkbRules"      "xorg"
#       Option          "XkbModel"      "pc105"
#       Option          "XkbLayout"     "us"   
#EndSection                                    

#Section "InputDevice"
#       Identifier      "Configured Mouse"
#       Driver          "mouse"           
#       Option          "CorePointer"     
#       Option          "Device"        "/dev/input/mice"
#       Option          "Protocol"      "ImPS/2"         
#       Option          "ZAxisMapping"  "4 5"            
#       Option          "Emulate3Buttons"       "true"   
#EndSection                                              

#Section "InputDevice"
#       Identifier      "Synaptics Touchpad"
#       Driver          "synaptics"         
#       Option          "SendCoreEvents"        "true"
#       Option          "Device"        "/dev/psaux"  
#       Option          "Protocol"      "auto-dev"    
#       Option          "VertEdgeScroll"        "1"   
#       Option          "VertTwoFingerScroll"   "1"   
#       Option          "HorizEdgeScroll"       "1"   
#       Option          "SHMConfig"     "true"        
#EndSection                                           

Section "InputDevice"
        Driver          "wacom"
        Identifier      "stylus"
        Option          "Device"        "/dev/input/wacom"
        Option          "Type"  "stylus"                  
        Option          "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection                                                     

Section "InputDevice"
        Driver          "wacom"
        Identifier      "eraser"
        Option          "Device"        "/dev/input/wacom"
        Option          "Type"  "eraser"                  
        Option          "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection                                                     

Section "InputDevice"
        Driver          "wacom"
        Identifier      "cursor"
        Option          "Device"        "/dev/input/wacom"
        Option          "Type"  "cursor"                  
        Option          "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection                                                     

Section "Device"
        Identifier      "nVidia Corporation NV34M [GeForce FX Go5200 32M/64M]"
        Driver          "nvidia"                                              
        Busid           "PCI:1:0:0"                                           
        Option          "AddARGBVisuals"        "True"                        
        Option          "AddARGBGLXVisuals"     "True"                        
        Option          "NoLogo"        "True"                                
        Option          "RandRRotation" "True"                                
        Option          "NvAgp"                 "1"                           
EndSection                                                                    

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier      "Generic Monitor"
        Option          "DPMS"           
        Horizsync       28-70            
        Vertrefresh     43-60            
EndSection                               

Section "Screen"
        Identifier      "Default Screen"
        Device          "nVidia Corporation NV34M [GeForce FX Go5200 32M/64M]"
        Monitor         "Generic Monitor"                                     
        Defaultdepth    24                                                    
        SubSection "Display"                                                  
                Modes           "1400x1050"
        EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
        Identifier      "Default Layout"
  screen "Default Screen"
#       Inputdevice     "Generic Keyboard"
#       Inputdevice     "Configured Mouse"

        # Uncomment if you have a wacom tablet
        Inputdevice     "stylus"        "SendCoreEvents"
        Inputdevice     "cursor"        "SendCoreEvents"
        Inputdevice     "eraser"        "SendCoreEvents"
#       Inputdevice     "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection
Section "Module"
        Load            "glx"
EndSection

```

---

### Post by Idire on 2009-03-17
Thanks for replying, but unfortunately it didn't have any effect.
(yes i reset the display manager with ctrl-alt-backspace)

Is there any diagnostics I can do?




The pen works on Windows XP / 7, so theres no problem with the hardware

---

### Post by solar george on 2009-03-17
Your /var/log/Xorg.* files may help. Also try reinstalling the default ubuntu wacom-tools and xserver-xorg-input-wacom packages.

---

### Post by Favux on 2009-03-17
Hi again Idire,

Then go ahead and try the attached xorg.conf.  The cursor stuff refers to the Wacom mouse for external graphics tablets so I removed it.  Be sure to back up your xorg.conf, and keep all your xorg.conf's (labeled) somewhere safe.  Compare the two to see what I did.

I'll also throw in some links for completeness sake:

[http://www.intilinux.com/]("http://www.intilinux.com/")

[http://risujin.org/debian/]("http://risujin.org/debian/")

By the way is it a Satellite or Protege M200?

### Post by Favux on 2009-03-18
Hi Idire,

The buttons are configured as:  first button is a middle mouse click and second button is a right mouse click.  If that isn't what you're getting, we can work with them.

Eraser doesn't just work every where, it has to be configured in the programs where it works.  In Xournal the right mouse click is the eraser (middle mouse click too I think).  More explanation on configuring the extended input devices in Gimp and Inkscape, etc. on the Wacom wiki link I gave above.


I don't know about rotation for your tablet.  Depends on the xrandr output.  See this thread:

[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=996830]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=996830")

By the way information on configuring the eraser is in post # 8 on the above linked thread.

---

### Post by Idire on 2009-03-18
> **Favux said:**
> Hi Idire,

The buttons are configured as:  first button is a middle mouse click and second button is a right mouse click.  If that isn't what you're getting, we can work with them.

Eraser doesn't just work every where, it has to be configured in the programs where it works.  In Xournal the right mouse click is the eraser (middle mouse click too I think).  More explanation on configuring the extended input devices in Gimp and Inkscape, etc. on the Wacom wiki link I gave above.


I don't know about rotation for your tablet.  Depends on the xrandr output.  See this thread:

[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=996830]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=996830")

By the way information on configuring the eraser is in post # 8 on the above linked thread.


Thanks, the write and erase work in Xournal, which suits me :)

Rotating the screen made the keyboard stop working, and the pen didnt track properly, and wouldnt click, which is a shame, but doesnt matter too much.

### Post by solar george on 2009-03-18
I use the following script (run at login) to setup the different pen buttons.
```
#!/bin/sh

# Setup eraser as middle mouse
xsetwacom set eraser button1 2
#Set barrel as right click
xsetwacom set stylus button2 3
#Set main back to left
xsetwacom set stylus button1 1


```

---

### Post by Idire on 2009-03-18
> **solar george said:**
> I use the following script (run at login) to setup the different pen buttons.
```
#!/bin/sh

# Setup eraser as middle mouse
xsetwacom set eraser button1 2
#Set barrel as right click
xsetwacom set stylus button2 3
#Set main back to left
xsetwacom set stylus button1 1


```


Could you explain how to set it to run on login? :)

---

### Post by solar george on 2009-03-18
Save that code in a .sh file (I have a directory called tablet in my home dir for all things to do with the tablet PC).
At a terminal do 
```
chmod +x *path_to_sh_file*
```
Or use the properties dialogue of your file manager to enable execution as a program.
You'll have to find the autostart managed on gnome (probably in the system > preferences menu) and add that script to it.
I'm afraid I'm not entirely sure how to do this under gnome as I tend to use KDE or Xfce.

---

### Post by Favux on 2009-03-18
Hi Idire,

The symptoms you are describing after rotation are usually associated with Compiz.  If it is disabled rotation usually starts working right.  What rotation script were you using?  If you used one of mine did you remove touch from it?

Usually you use xorg.conf to set buttons.  You'll see the xsetwacom version of the setting in your .xinitrc if you've used "wacomcpl" to calibrate your stylus.  See section 3 (and appendix 2) here:

[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=6546012#post6546012]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=6546012#post6546012")

on how to use wacomcpl.  Remember .xinitrc will be a hidden file in your /home/username directory.  You can also use xsetwacom to configure the buttons on the "fly" in a terminal.  Or you could use a shell script to run before starting a particular application.

---

### Post by solar george on 2009-04-26
Here's an updated version of the stylus script for Jaunty
```
#!/bin/sh

# Setup eraser as middle mouse
xsetwacom set "Wacom Serial Tablet PC Pen Tablet/Digitizer eraser" button1 2
#Set barrel as right click
xsetwacom set "Wacom Serial Tablet PC Pen Tablet/Digitizer" button2 3
#Set main back to left
xsetwacom set "Wacom Serial Tablet PC Pen Tablet/Digitizer" button1 1


```
And an xorg.conf also for Jaunty (using restricted drivers)
```
Section "Screen"
        Identifier      "Default Screen"
        DefaultDepth    24              
        Option  "AddARGBGLXVisuals"     "True"
EndSection

Section "Module"
        Load    "glx"
EndSection

Section "Device"
        Identifier      "Default Device"
        Driver  "nvidia"
        Option  "NoLogo"        "True"
        Option  "RandRRotation" "True"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
        Option  "DontZap"       "False"
EndSection


```

---

### Post by Favux on 2010-06-20
Hi majedaly,

As I recall there was a bug with 64-bit Karmic (9.10).  To get around it after booting restart X.  At least that seemed to work for some.

See:
[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=8478200&highlight=t4010#post8478200](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=8478200&highlight=t4010#post8478200)
[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=1038949&page=66](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=1038949&page=66)

So update your Karmic if you haven't already.  I suppose you could try a newer linuxwacom like 0.8.6-2 also.  See the [linuxwacom HOW TO]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=6546012&postcount=1").

Your xorg.conf could use a little clean up.  I'm not sure about the eraser.  I think some t4010's had them.

---
